event_world_42_20260423_084257_0000.name: Artisan Crafts & Vintage Fair
event_world_42_20260423_084257_0000.description: Over 200 stalls of handmade jewellery, vintage clothing, rare books, and bespoke homeware from independent makers across the region.

reviews.text: Solid community event with a genuine local flavour — not flashy but heartfelt.
Great charity component to the event — we felt our attendance actually contributed to something positive.
Decent event overall though the merchandise stall ran out of programmes by midday.
The street food village was as good as the performances; we spent the whole day eating our way around.
Terrible organisation — queues stretched around the block and we missed the first act entirely.
Solid community event with a genuine local flavour — not flashy but heartfelt.

event_world_42_20260423_084257_0001.name: Farm-to-Table Harvest Dinner
event_world_42_20260423_084257_0001.description: Held on an actual working farm, this five-course dinner uses ingredients harvested that very morning, prepared by a rotating cast of local chefs.

reviews.text: Long waits at every food and drink stall; the cashless payment system crashed repeatedly.
Great selection of local craft vendors; the live folk music added a lovely ambient backdrop.

event_world_42_20260423_084257_0002.name: Hamlet: Reimagined
event_world_42_20260423_084257_0002.description: A boundary-pushing director stages Shakespeare's Hamlet in a dystopian near-future, with a gender-swapped cast and an original score by an electronic composer.

reviews.text: The fireworks finale was extraordinary — timed perfectly to the closing score.
Intimate venue setting meant even the cheap tickets gave a brilliant view of the stage.
Well-organised queuing system with multiple bag-check points; security was thorough but fast.
Half the scheduled activities were moved indoors with no explanation; the outdoor programme was gutted.

event_world_42_20260423_084257_0003.name: K-pop Galaxy Tour
event_world_42_20260423_084257_0003.description: The world-sensation aespa is in town! Get ready for an unforgettable night of dynamic electronic dance music and stunning holographic visuals.

reviews.text: Seating was first-come-first-served despite tickets having numbered seats printed on them — total chaos.
Electric atmosphere from start to finish — the performance was world-class.
Ticket prices do not reflect the actual production values — feels like a premium charge for a mid-tier experience.
Too many commercial sponsors with their logos dominating every surface — completely killed the atmosphere.
The app kept showing wrong tent locations and staff gave contradictory directions.
